title: Special measures for emergencies in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
Immigration, citizenship and passports

## Special measures for domestic natural disasters in Canada
As part of the Government of Canada’s support for people across the country who have been impacted by natural disasters, such as wildfires, floods and hurricanes, we act quickly to help people get the immigration, passport and citizenship support they need.
If you are impacted by a situation outside Canada

## Ongoing special measures
- 2026 to 2028: Special measure for students, workers and visitors affected by domestic natural disasters

## Past special measures
- 2025 – Wildfires in Canada: Temporary immigration, passport and citizenship measures
- 2024 – Wildfires in Canada: Temporary immigration, passport and citizenship measures
- 2023 – Wildfires in Canada: Temporary immigration, passport and citizenship measures
- 2023 – Floods in Nova Scotia – 2023: Temporary immigration, passport and citizenship measures
- 2023 – Hurricane Fiona: Special measures
- 2021 – Floods in British Columbia: Special measures
- 2017 – Wildfires in British Columbia: Special measures
